summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 6 warning, 12 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:128-132` — Monkey-patching `process.exit()` creates hidden coupling to `runSingleFeature`'s internals. Add backlog item to refactor `runSingleFeature` to accept `{ throwOnError: true }` instead of calling `process.exit()`.
🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:159` — Path sanitization regex only strips macOS `/Users/...` paths. Linux `/home/...` paths would leak to GitHub comments. Broaden regex before cross-platform use.
🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:159` — Path sanitization regex only covers macOS paths (`/Users/...`). Linux paths (`/home/...`) would leak into GitHub issue comments. Pre-existing gap — should go to backlog.

🟡 `test/cron-tick.test.mjs:59` — `afterEach` restores `process.cwd` and `process.exit` but not `console.error`/`console.log`; if a test fails unexpectedly mid-patch, the monkey-patched console persists for remaining tests. Add console restoration to `afterEach` for defensive cleanup.

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:159` — Path sanitization regex `/\/Users\/[^\s:]+/g` is macOS-specific. Linux paths (`/home/`, `/var/`) and CI paths (`/runner/`, `/github/workspace/`) would leak into GitHub issue comments on public repos. Should go to backlog.

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:128-132` — The `process.exit` monkey-patch is inherently fragile. If `runSingleFeature` adds a blanket `try/catch` that swallows the intercepted throw, board state stays in-progress without recovery. Should document this coupling constraint.
